differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus with Libman-Sacks endocarditis
Disseminated malignancy with nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis
next_steps: Testing for antiphospholipid antibodies (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I)
Serum ADAMTS13 activity and inhibitor level
Blood culture and specialized culture/PCR testing for slow-growing endocarditis organisms (e.g., Bartonella, Coxiella, Tropheryma whipplei)
Comprehensive autoimmune panel including dsDNA and complement levels
Neurology consultation for management of hemorrhagic and ischemic cerebrovascular lesions

differential_diagnoses: Infective endocarditis (culture-negative, likely fastidious organism e.g., Bartonella spp.)
Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with Libman-Sacks endocarditis
Nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis (NBTE) associated with hypercoagulable state
Disseminated fungal infection (e.g., Histoplasmosis or Aspergillosis)
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to assess for valvular vegetations
Send serum for comprehensive antiphospholipid antibody panel (Lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I)
Order specialized serologies and PCR for culture-negative endocarditis (Bartonella, Coxiella, Tropheryma, Brucella)
Assess for hemolytic anemia markers (peripheral blood smear for schistocytes, haptoglobin, LDH) and obtain ADAMTS13 activity
Perform serum complement levels (C3/C4) and repeat autoimmune panel (dsDNA, ENA)
Initiate serial blood cultures and potentially valve surgery consultation pending TEE findings
